question.text: When a mutual fund scheme undergoes a fundamental attribute change (such as change in investment objective or scheme type), what right does the investor have?

answer.text: Right to vote against the change at a unitholder meeting
Right to exit the scheme without any exit load within a prescribed period after being notified of the change
Right to switch to any other AMC's scheme at no cost
Right to receive additional units as compensation for the change

explanation.text: **The Logic:** When a scheme's fundamental attributes are changed (investment objective, type, asset allocation pattern, etc.), investors must be notified and given the right to exit the scheme without any exit load within a prescribed period (typically 30 days from the notice). This protects investors from being locked into a scheme that no longer matches their original investment thesis. **The Trap:** While unitholders may vote on some changes through special resolution, the key RIGHT tested here is the exit-without-load option. Investors can't block the change but can leave without penalty. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A covers fundamental attribute changes; Series II-B tests the RTA's role in processing exit-load-free redemptions during the prescribed window. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Monitor fundamental attribute change notifications carefully — they're rare but significant. Proactively reach out to affected clients with your analysis of whether the changed scheme still suits their portfolio. The load-free exit window is time-limited, so quick action is essential.
